name: ControlledChaos
description: An ADHD-friendly task manager built around brain dumps (text, voice, or photo) that Claude Haiku parses into structured tasks. Crisis mode for overwhelm days, AI-powered prioritization that factors in time and energy, and Parallel Play rooms for body-doubling with friends. One-tap Moments capture energy and focus state; microtasks surface small recurring prompts without streak guilt. Includes Canvas calendar sync, location-aware notifications, momentum visualization, and a Mirror view that shows your whole day in one chronological timeline. Targeting public launch June 2026.

name: ChaosLimbă
tagline: English-to-Romanian language learning grounded in SLA research.
description: A CALL app for English speakers learning Romanian, designed for ADHD brains and grounded in second language acquisition theory. Built around a 10-component AI ensemble with fossilization detection that turns persistent errors into micro-curriculum. Targeting MVP launch August 2026.

name: ChaosLengua
description: Spanish-language sister project to ChaosLimbă, built in a shared monorepo with the same SLA-grounded AI ensemble architecture and Adaptation Engine. Shares Clerk auth and monorepo tooling with ChaosLimbă while targeting Spanish-specific error patterns like ser/estar and preterite/imperfect.

name: ChaosCitim
tagline: Romanian reading companion with graduated morphological glossing.
description: The reading-side counterpart to ChaosLimbă: a PWA for reading authentic Romanian texts with tier-based glossing that escalates only when needed. Tracks what learners resolve unaided to feed a single cross-modal interlanguage model spanning reading and production.

name: Cha(t)os
description: Real-time chat rooms where multiple users each bring their own Claude — with their own persona, system prompt, and BYOK API key. Claudes respond to @mentions and can talk to each other in sequence, enabling genuine AI-to-AI dialogue. Personal Context MCP layers per-user identity into every Claude.

name: ChaosPatch
tagline: Patch tracker built for agentic development workflows.
description: A PWA for tracking dev patches — small specs that Claude Code can pick up and implement. Integrated via MCP so patches flow directly into Claude Code sessions instead of dying in a Notion doc. Includes batch updates, cross-project search, and an Insights page that visualizes work-in-flight across projects.

name: ScribeCat
description: Records lectures with real-time AssemblyAI transcription, generates AI study notes mid-recording (not after), and includes six AI study tools (flashcards, quizzes, concept maps, ELI5). Features exam study rooms with weak-spot tracking, a Tamagotchi-style pixel cat (StudyQuest) tied to study metrics, multiplayer Quiz Battle and Jeopardy, friends + DMs, and a Chrome extension that imports Canvas courses.

name: ThreadNotes
tagline: Personal research command center for the way ADHD brains actually research.
description: A unified hub for academic research: search peer-reviewed literature via OpenAlex and Crossref, automatically discover free open-access PDFs via Unpaywall, save and annotate articles, organize research questions by theme with bidirectional article linking, write reflective journal entries, and get AI-generated summaries. Includes a Chrome extension for clipping excerpts and an MCP server so Claude can read and write to your research base.

name: ThreadBrain
tagline: Reading companion that scaffolds dense text for ADHD brains.
description: AI-generated micro-headers, purpose-aware chunking that adapts to your stated reading goal and energy level, and "The Map" — a 2-3 sentence orientation summary before reading begins. Includes ExpressBrain for cram-sheet generation and integrates with ThreadNotes so excerpts flow into your research thread.

name: ChickenScratch
tagline: Editorial portal for Hen & Ink Society literary zine.
description: The submissions and review portal for Hen & Ink Society, the first AAP-based RSO at the University of Delaware. Handles submission intake, editor review, and publishing workflows for the zine.

name: Personal Context MCP
description: An MCP server that stores identity, projects, relationships, and preferences for cross-session Claude context. Powers Cha(t)os context awareness and provides continuity across Claude conversations. Multi-tenant with Redis-backed persistence.

name: Tangle
description: An MCP server that persists Claude's epistemic state across sessions — hunches, suspicions, dead-ends, and partial reasoning that would otherwise evaporate at session end. Sibling to Personal Context MCP (which captures identity and projects) and the built-in memory feature (which captures chat history); Tangle captures the texture of working on a problem. Each Claude attributes their notes via an identity header or URL parameter, and a recency × confidence ranking surfaces what matters most when re-entering a project.
